mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating consumer spending power and rising recession probability, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to produce a positive mean 20-day return, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against a univariate baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side slack, which suppresses corporate profit margins and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production contractions, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2.5 percent, monetary policy enters an accommodative zone that stimulates risk appetite and liquidity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS drops below 2.5, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer confidence, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative or zero 5-day forward returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from noise in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in real GDP growth signal weakening aggregate demand and lower corporate revenue trajectories, which systematically reduce S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no negative correlation with GDPC1 declines, or the predictive IC remains below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production exceeds 110, manufacturing capacity utilization approaches peak levels, creating supply bottlenecks that compress margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 60% of periods when INDPRO surpasses 110, or the threshold signal fails to beat a naive momentum benchmark.
